seoTitle: Change the Currency Symbol in Power BI Quickly
metaDescription: Learn how to change the currency symbol in Power BI in a few clicks, so financial reports display the right format for your audience.
intro: The wrong currency symbol on a financial report can confuse stakeholders or make numbers look inaccurate at a glance, and fixing it in Power BI is quicker than most people expect. This guide walks through the settings that control currency formatting and how to apply the right symbol to your visuals. It's a short, practical fix for anyone reporting on multi-country or region-specific financial data.

steps.heading: Understand currency settings in Power BI
steps.body: Currency symbols are tied to a field's format settings, which you can adjust at the column level or within an individual visual.

steps.heading: Change the symbol step by step
steps.body: Select the field, open its format settings, and choose the correct currency type or enter a custom format string to display the symbol you need.

steps.heading: Consider use cases for multiple currencies
steps.body: If you report across different countries, apply currency formatting per relevant table or visual so each region displays its correct symbol.

steps.heading: Explore integration with other automation tools
steps.body: Once set, the currency format persists across refreshes, and can be reused in templates so you don't have to reconfigure it for every new report.

faq.question: Can I set a custom currency symbol not in Power BI's default list?
faq.answer: Yes, you can use a custom format string in the field formatting options to display symbols or codes not included in Power BI's built-in currency list.

faq.question: Does changing the currency symbol convert the actual values?
faq.answer: No, changing the symbol only affects how the number displays — it does not perform any currency conversion on the underlying values.

faq.question: Can different visuals show different currency symbols in the same report?
faq.answer: Yes, since currency formatting can be applied at the field or visual level, different parts of the same report can display different currencies as needed.

faq.question: Will the currency format carry over if I duplicate the report?
faq.answer: Yes, formatting applied to fields and visuals is saved with the report, so duplicating it keeps the currency symbol intact.
